title: pastinaca latifolia
intro: Pastinaca latifolia, also known as wild parsnip, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of wild parsnip include reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as potentially treating digestive issues and skin problems. Wild parsnip can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts, and its seeds and roots can be used in tinctures or as a culinary ingredient.
